description_blurb: Each of these incident summaries includes a "Check-List - Unidentified Flying Objects" that contains details about the incident. Many summaries also include witness lists or statements and other narrative reports or descriptions.
title: 38_143685_box7_Incident_Summaries_1-100
incident_location: Various locations in California, Oregon, Washington, and Idaho, USA
summary: The document compiles multiple UAP incident summaries from July 1947 across California, Oregon, Washington, and Idaho. Witnesses, including military personnel, police officers, and pilots, reported disc-like, spherical, or ovular objects exhibiting high speeds, silent flight (except one low humming sound), and erratic maneuvers such as tight circles, oscillations, and sudden directional changes. Many sightings were corroborated by multiple observers.
reported_object_description: Objects were described as disc-like, spherical, ovular, or saucer-shaped. Colors included silver, yellowish-white, white, aluminum, chromium, and whitish-brown. Sizes ranged from 5-10 feet in diameter to about 50 feet. Some appeared metallic, one had two projections (fins or nobs) on its upper surface, and another was described as thin and smooth on the bottom, rough on top. One formation was "V" shaped like a flock of geese.
reported_behavior: Objects exhibited high speeds (up to 400 MPH, "terrific speed"), horizontal flight, tight circles, oscillating, whirling, dipping up and down, wobbling, disappearing and reappearing, and changing direction at 90-degree angles. Some maintained straight courses, others flew in loose or straight-line formations. One object drifted against the prevailing wind. Most were silent, but one reported a low humming sound.
official_resolution: Confirmed by other sources; corroborated accounts.
bust_reasoning: The bust score is low (20) because the document details multiple incidents with strong multi-witness convergence across different locations (Muroc and Oregon) and dates, all within a short timeframe. Witnesses, including military personnel and police officers (some with aviation experience), consistently describe silent, disc-like or spherical objects exhibiting highly anomalous kinematics for 1947, such as 'terrific speed,' 'tight circles' incompatible with known aircraft, '90-degree angle without difficulty,' and movement 'against prevailing wind.' The document itself does not offer mundane explanations, and witnesses actively rule out common prosaic sources like weather balloons or conventional aircraft based on observed behavior.

# 38_143685_box7_Incident_Summaries_1-100 - Page 3

~~CONFIDENTIAL~~

enroute to his office and before entering heard one of the local aircraft in the traffic pattern. Looking up he observed the aircraft and slightly to the left he saw 2 silver objects of either spherical or disc-like shape moving about 300 MPH at approximately 8000 ft heading about 320° due north. He immediately called S/Sgt Gerald E. Nauman, T/Sgt Joseph Ruvolo and Miss Jannette Marie Scotte. Witnesses all verified the sighting and the direction of the objects - all three stated that the objects were moving toward Mojave, Calif.

Witness was sure it was not an optical illusion or that the objects were not weather balloons since they traveled against prevailing wind and since the speed at which they traveled and the horizontal direction taken indicated that they were not weather balloons. Furthermore they could not remain at the same altitude so consistently if they were weather balloons.
Witness then attempted to get personnel (medical officers) to further verify the sighting but by the time the additional personnel had arrived at the some the original objects had disappeared due to the speed at which they were traveling. However, two of them sighted a third object of a silver spherical or disc-like nature at approximately 8000 ft traveling in circles over the north end of the airfield. Five out of the seven personnel saw this object All looked away from the object several times to make sure there was no eye strain. He stated that this object performed too tight a circle to be any type of known aircraft.

Evaluation: Confirmed by other sources.

Witnesses: S/Sgt Gerald E. Nauman
T/Sgt Joseph Ruvolo
Miss Jannette Marie Scotte
Pfc T. C. [illegible]

~~CONFIDENTIAL~~

2

# 38_143685_box7_Incident_Summaries_1-100 - Page 5

~~CONFIDENTIAL~~

Joseph Ruvolo and S/Sgt. Gerald E. Nauman to the front of the B.O.Q. "A" Bldg. Pointing up in a direction directly above the group he asked them to explain what they saw. Witness states that she saw 2 silver-colored disc-like objects flying toward Mojave, Calif, one directly back of the other, at a speed of about 300 to 400 MPH at an altitude of approximately 8,000 ft. She listened carefully for a few minutes but could hear no drone such as occasioned by any aircraft. Casting her eyes for a moment in another direction and then looking back to the same spot she was able to distinguish the same objects again. Assured that it was not eye-strain, she was convinced the objects were not weather balloons due to the horizontal position in which they traveled. Nor could they have been birds dince they reflected the sun's rays.

Some three or four mimues after the objects had nearly disappeared she saw another flying object similar to the above-mentioned objects. It was silver colored and disc-like in shape. Unlike the first two, it flew in a tight circle, neither losing nor gaining altitude, at approximately 8,000 ft. She thought it performed too tight a circle to have been any type aircraft with which she was familiar.

Evaluation: Confirmed by other sources

~~CONFIDENTIAL~~

# 38_143685_box7_Incident_Summaries_1-100 - Page 7

~~CONFIDENTIAL~~

About 0945 Lt McHenry, Billeting Officer in Charge, upon returning from the Post Exchange contacted the witness, Sgt Ruvol and asked him to look up in the sky. Witness observed two (2) flying objects which appeared like two flying discs or saucer-shaped silver-colored objects flying in a northwestern direction at approximately 350 to 400 MPH at an altitude of about 7500 or 8000 ft. He could hear no motor roar and was positive that it was not a balloon.

He states: "I am of good health and sound mind and this was no hallucination"

Evaluation: Confirmed by other sources.

~~CONFIDENTIAL~~

# 38_143685_box7_Incident_Summaries_1-100 - Page 10

~~CONFIDENTIAL~~

REMARKS :

Statement given by Major J. C. Wise, Test Pilot, Muroc Army Air Field, Muroc, California.

On 7 July 1947, at approximately 10:10, while running up the XP-84 on the ground I noticed everyone was looking up into the air. Off to the north about 10,000 to 12,000 feet altitude, was an object that I assumed at first to be a weather balloon, but after looking at it for a while I noticed that it was oscillating in a forward whirling movement without losing altitude. It was traveling about 200 to 225 MPH, and heading from west to east.

The object was yellowish white in color and I would estimate that it was a sphere about 5 to 10 feet in diameter.

CONFIDENTIAL

# 38_143685_box7_Incident_Summaries_1-100 - Page 12

~~CONFIDENTIAL~~

On the 8 July 1947 at 11:50 the undersigned was sitting in an observation truck located in Area #3, Rogers Dry Lake, was gazing upward toward a formation of two (2) P-82's and an A-26 aircraft fly ing at 20,000 feet, preparing the carry out of a seat ejection experiment, when I observed a rounded object, white aluminum in color, which at first resembled a parachute canopy. The first impression was that a premature ejection of the seat and dummy had occurred. This body was ejected at a determined height lower than 20,000 feet, and was falling at three (3) times the rate observed for the parachute which ejected thirty minutes later. As it fell it drifted slightly north of due west aganist the prevailing wind, toward Mount Wilson. The speed, horizontal motion could not be determined, but appeared slower than the maximum velocity P-80 aircraft.

As this object descended through a low enough level to permit observation of its lateral silhouette, it presented a distinct ovular outline, with two (2) projections on the upper surface which might have been thich fins or nobs. These crossed each other at intervals, suggesting either rotation or oscillation of slow type.

No smoke flames, propellar arks, engine noise, or other clasuable or visible mens of propulsion were noted. The color was silvery, resembling aluminum painted fabric, and did not appear as dense as a parachute canopy.

When the object dropped to a level such that comes into line of vision of the mountain tops, it was lost to the vision of the observer.

It is estimated that the object was in line of vision about 90 seconds. Of the five (5) people sitting in the observation truck, four (4) observed this object and made remarks about it. These people include:

Mr. Lenz - Civilian, Wright Field, Dayton, Ohio.
(Other names not given)

The following is my own personal opinions about this object:

1. I think it was a manmade object, as evidenced distaintly by the outline and functional appearance.

2. It's size was not far from 25 feet with a parachute canopy.

3. The path followed by this object appeared as though it might have been dropped from a great height.

Seeing this was not a hallucination or other fancies of a sense.

~~CONFIDENTIAL~~

# 38_143685_box7_Incident_Summaries_1-100 - Page 14

CONFIDENTIAL

Officer McDowell stated that at approximately 1305, 4 July 1947 while he was on duty at Precinct #1 and feeding the pigeons in the parking lot back of the station he noticed that the pigeons became quite excited over something and fluttered in the air. Officer McDowell in looking around to see what had disturbed them saw 5 large discs in the air east of Portland; two discs flying south and three flying in an easterly direction. He stated he could determine no color to the discs but advised they were dipping in an up and down oscillating motion and were traveling at great speed. He was unable to give an estimate of the speed or altitude of the discs as they were out of sight before any detailed observation could be made. Officer McDowell advised that he notified the Police Radio who immediately broadcast an alert. He saw no indication of any motivating force nor heard any sound coming from the discs and could give no description other than round.

CONFIDENTIAL

# RESTRICTED

Witness was getting out of his car when he observed one disc flying in a Southwesterly direction over Portland. Disc was aluminum in color and traveled at terrific speed - faster than any flying object witness had ever seen - It left no vapor trail or smoke trail. ~~It was traveling at terrific speed~~, Estimated altitude approx 30,000 ft. Thought the disc was definitely some type of aircraft but could not give further description as speed made observation difficult. No sound was heard from the flying object. He thought it appeared radio-controlled because the disc could change direction at a 90° angle without difficulty.

RESTRICTED

Patrolman W. A. Lissy of the Portland City Police Department possesses a private pilot license. He perceived three flat round discs which flew at terrific speed in straight line formation the last disc fluttering very rapidly in a side-way arc. They saw no evidence of any motivating powers and there were no vapor or smoke trails. No sound could be heard. Ground temperature was 82°F with little or no cloud formation.

NOTE: Corroroborated account. Witnessed by Patrolman D. W. Ellis (also a private pilot) Both very dependable & trustworthy officers.

RESTRICTED

# 38_143685_box7_Incident_Summaries_1-100 - Page 24

RESTRICTED

Members of the harbor patrol at the foot of NW Irving Street stepped out when they heard the all-car alert. Capt. K. A. Prehn, Harbor Pilot, A.T. Austed and Patrolman K. C. Hoff, all saw the objects and said they appeared to be going south high over the Glove Mills at terrific speed. Capt. Prehn said the flashes kept them from ascertaining whether there were three or six.

"The discs would oscillate and sometimes we would see a full disc, then a half-moon shape, then nothing at all," he reported. "The objects looked more like a shiny chromium hub cap off a car which wobbled, disappeared and reappeared."

There was a plane in the sky at the time, but all were emphatic that the discs were not planes.

RELIABILITY: Corroborated report

RESTRICTED

# 38_143685_box7_Incident_Summaries_1-100 - Page 26

CONFIDENTIAL

Capt E. J. Smith, UAL Pilot, enroute to Seattle reported that he and the entire crew of the westbound UAL plane saw nine flying discs near Emmett, Idaho. At first he saw 5 discs flying what appeared to be a "loose formation." They called Marty Morrow, Stewardess, to the cockpit to verify that they were actually seeing the discs. She saw them too. Then they saw 4 more of them, three cluster together, and a 4th flying by itself, way off in the distance.

Capt Smith described them as follows:

5 "somethings" which were "thin and smooth on the bottom and rough appearing on top." Silhouetted against sunset shortly after the plane took off at 8:04 p. m. "We saw them clearly," he reported. "We followed them in a northwesterly direction for about 45 miles. Finally they disappeared. We were unable to tell whether they outsped the plane or disintegrated."

He couldn't determine the shape "We can't say whether they were saucer-like, oval or anything else." "But whatever they were, they were not other aircraft, nor were they smoke or clouds."

RELIABILITY: Corroborated report by a UAL Pilot

cover_up_reasoning: The document shows substantial evidence of past concealment. It was originally classified SECRET, then downgraded to CONFIDENTIAL, and eventually DECLASSIFIED. Specific redactions of names and phrases, along with references to 'confirmed by other sources' without elaboration, indicate that information was withheld or restricted from public view.
summary_zh: 本文件汇编了1947年7月在加利福尼亚州、俄勒冈州、华盛顿州和爱达荷州发生的多起UAP事件摘要。目击者包括军事人员、警官和飞行员，他们报告称目睹了盘状、球状或卵状物体，这些物体表现出高速飞行、无声飞行（除一次低沉的嗡嗡声外）以及急转弯、摆动和突然改变方向等不规则机动。多起目击事件得到多名观察员的证实。
reported_object_description_zh: 物体被描述为盘状、球状、卵状或碟状。颜色包括银色、黄白色、白色、铝色、铬色和灰白色。尺寸从直径5-10英尺到约50英尺不等。有些呈金属质感，其中一个上表面有两处突出物（鳍状或球状），另一个被描述为底部薄而光滑，顶部粗糙。一个编队呈“V”字形，如同雁群。
reported_behavior_zh: 物体表现出高速（最高达400英里/小时，“惊人的速度”）、水平飞行、急转弯、摆动、旋转、上下俯冲、摇晃、消失与再现，以及90度角改变方向。有些保持直线航向，另一些则以松散或直线编队飞行。一个物体逆着盛行风漂移。大多数无声，但一次报告有低沉的嗡嗡声。
official_resolution_zh: 经其他来源证实；证词相互印证。
bust_reasoning_zh: 驳斥得分较低（20分），因为该文件详细描述了在短时间内，不同地点（穆罗克和俄勒冈州）和日期发生的多起事件，这些事件具有强烈的多目击者证词一致性。目击者，包括军事人员和警官（部分具有航空经验），一致描述了无声的盘状或球状物体，这些物体在1947年表现出高度异常的运动学特征，例如“惊人的速度”、“与已知飞机不符的急转弯”、“毫不费力地90度角转向”以及“逆风移动”。文件本身并未提供世俗解释，且目击者根据观察到的行为，主动排除了气象气球或常规飞机等常见的普通来源。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 该文件显示了过去存在大量隐瞒证据。它最初被列为“绝密”，随后降级为“机密”，最终解密。对姓名和短语的具体编辑，以及在未详细说明的情况下提及“经其他来源证实”，均表明信息曾被扣留或限制公众查阅。
incident_location_zh: 美国加利福尼亚州、俄勒冈州、华盛顿州和爱达荷州多地
claims: Multiple disc-like, spherical, or ovular objects observed
Objects traveled at high speeds, estimated up to 400 MPH
Objects performed erratic maneuvers including tight circles, oscillations, and 90-degree turns
Most objects were silent; one reported a low humming sound
Objects were metallic, silver, white, yellowish-white, aluminum, or chromium in color
Objects were not identified as known aircraft or weather balloons
Many sightings were corroborated by multiple witnesses
claims_zh: 观察到多个盘状、球状或卵状物体
物体以高速飞行，估计最高达400英里/小时
物体进行了不规则机动，包括急转弯、摆动和90度转向
大多数物体无声；一次报告有低沉的嗡嗡声
物体呈金属质感，颜色为银色、白色、黄白色、铝色或铬色
物体未被识别为已知飞机或气象气球
多次目击事件得到多名目击者的证实
bust_explanations: Advanced (but unknown to public) experimental aircraft/drones: Given the Muroc Air Field location and test pilot witnesses, it's plausible that highly experimental craft were being tested. However, the described silent, high-speed, disc-shaped objects with 90-degree turns and movement against prevailing winds are highly unusual for 1947, even for experimental craft.

High-altitude balloons (misidentified despite witness claims): Although witnesses explicitly ruled out weather balloons due to observed speed, horizontal flight, and movement against wind, and one observer initially assumed a balloon but behavior contradicted it, this remains a common default explanation for unidentified aerial phenomena.

Unusual atmospheric phenomena / mirages: While less likely to produce consistent disc/spherical shapes with directed, high-speed movement and sharp turns across multiple independent observers, atmospheric conditions could potentially cause visual distortions or reflections that contribute to some sightings.
bust_explanations_zh: 先进（但公众未知）的实验性飞机/无人机：考虑到穆罗克机场的位置和试飞员目击者，高度实验性的飞行器正在测试是合理的。然而，所描述的无声、高速、碟状物体，具有90度转向和逆风移动的特征，即使对于1947年的实验性飞行器来说，也是高度异常的。
高空气球（尽管目击者声称已排除，但仍被误认）：尽管目击者因观察到的速度、水平飞行和逆风移动而明确排除了气象气球，且一名观察员最初认为是气球但其行为与此相悖，但这仍然是UAP的常见默认解释。
异常大气现象/海市蜃楼：虽然不太可能在多名独立观察员之间产生具有定向、高速移动和急转弯的连贯盘状/球状形状，但大气条件可能导致视觉扭曲或反射，从而促成某些目击事件。
cover_up_indicators: CONFIDENTIAL with SECRET stamped over and crossed out on page 1
Other names not given for witnesses on page 12
Evaluation: Confirmed by other sources without detailing those sources
cover_up_indicators_zh: 第1页上盖有“绝密”并划掉，改为“机密”
第1页上“[已编辑] [已编辑] [已编辑]”
第12页未提供其他目击者姓名
评估：经其他来源证实，但未详细说明这些来源
文件密级历史（绝密 -> 机密 -> 解密）

locations.location_text: Various locations in California, Oregon, Washington, and Idaho, USA
